creator: synthetic-social-lab
hook_text: Paraphrase: vague plans keep promising friendships stuck as acquaintances.
opening_visual: A calendar invitation appears beside the speaker.
premise: Specific time-and-place invitations lower the ambiguity of making plans.
audience_job: turn an acquaintance into a friend
reusable_script_pattern: Pain point -> counterintuitive reframe -> 3-step experiment -> save CTA
analyst_inference: The concrete invitation makes the advice immediately testable.

creator: synthetic-social-lab
hook_text: Paraphrase: being interesting may matter less than showing interest.
opening_visual: A two-column comparison contrasts performing and listening.
premise: A useful follow-up question responds to the detail the other person just offered.
reusable_script_pattern: Research claim -> plain-language meaning -> one practice prompt -> share CTA
research_claims: People who ask more follow-up questions may be rated as more likable in conversation.
analyst_inference: The reversal gives the viewer a memorable behavior to test.
notes: Synthetic demonstration record; research claim is illustrative and not externally verified here.

creator: synthetic-social-lab
hook_text: Paraphrase: a pause is not proof that the conversation failed.
opening_visual: A staged conversation freezes after a short silence.
premise: Naming the pause or returning to a prior detail can restart a stalled exchange.
reusable_script_pattern: Pain point -> counterintuitive reframe -> 3-step experiment -> save CTA
analyst_inference: The skit lowers the emotional cost of practicing the response.

creator: synthetic-social-lab
hook_text: Paraphrase: repeat a name with meaning, not as a memory trick.
opening_visual: Three names are attached to three visual details.
premise: Connecting a name to one observed detail creates a stronger retrieval cue.
cta: practice on the next three introductions
reusable_script_pattern: Challenge -> live drill -> memory cue -> repeat
analyst_inference: The compact drill is easy to rehearse without extra tools.

creator: synthetic-social-lab
hook_text: Paraphrase: proving competence first can make connection harder.
opening_visual: Warmth and competence swap positions in an on-screen ranking.
premise: A warm opening can reduce uncertainty before a person evaluates competence.
cta: share with someone starting a new room
reusable_script_pattern: Research claim -> plain-language meaning -> one practice prompt -> share CTA
research_claims: People often evaluate warmth before competence when forming first impressions.
analyst_inference: The counterintuitive ordering creates a strong reason to keep watching.
notes: Synthetic demonstration record; research claim is illustrative and not externally verified here.
